# Export-readonly command implementation (2026-03-13 06:18Z)

_**User**_

Implement Task 16 from docs/plans/2026-03-13-dev-shell-impl-plan.md.

**Task 16: export-readonly 内置命令**

1. In src/vfs.rs add a helper (e.g. copy_tree_to_host) or in command.rs: given a Vfs and a path (default / or cwd), recursively copy that subtree to a host directory. For each Dir create a directory; for each File write file content. Use std::fs::create_dir_all and std::fs::write. Paths must be safe (no .. escaping the export root).

2. In src/command.rs add "export-readonly" (or "export_readonly") as a builtin: when argv[0] == "export-readonly", take optional path (argv[1], default "/" or cwd). Create a temp dir with std::env::temp_dir() and a unique subdir name (e.g. "dev_shell_export_" + random or timestamp). Copy the vfs subtree to that dir. Write the temp dir's absolute path to stdout (so user can use it). Return Ok(()).

3. Add test: run export-readonly, check temp dir exists and contains expected file/dir (e.g. after mkdir /x and write_file /x/f with "hi", export and assert the host path has x/f with content "hi").

4. Run cargo test. Commit: git add src/command.rs src/vfs.rs tests/command_tests.rs && git commit -m "feat(command): export-readonly to host temp dir"

Work directory: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/dev_shell
